original: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope launched in joint effort with ESA (European Space Agency) and the Canadian Space Agency on an Ariane 5 rocket from Europe’s Spaceport in French Guiana, South America
Japanese scientist achieve -271.8°C, coldest temperature ever recorded
Cassini orbiter releases Huygens probe which successfully landed on Saturn's moon Titan on January 14, 2005.
Legendary "Christmas Truce" takes place on the battlefields of WWI between British and German troops. Instead of fighting, soldiers exchange gifts and play football
American Revolutionary War:George Washingtoncrosses the Delaware River into New Jersey, surprises and defeats 1,400 Hessians
Ed Smylie [Robert Smylie], American NASA engineer who led the team that saved the Apollo 13 mission, born in Lincoln County, Mississippi (d. 2025)[1]
Rare polar rain aurora, stretching for 3,000 km, is visible across the North Pole, caused by charged particles flowing from the Sun
1st pictures of a comet from space (Comet Kohoutek), taken by NASA astronauts Jerry Carr and William Pogue during a spacewalk from Skylab 4
Christopher Columbus' flagship the Santa María runs aground and sinks on the north coast of Hispaniola. The crew are left to found a colony as Columbus returns to Spain
Cyclone Tracy virtually destroys Darwin, Australia
The ARPANET crashes when a programming bug causes all ARPANET traffic to be routed through the server at Harvard University, causing the server to freeze
Dutch astronomer and mathematicianChristiaan Huygenscreates the first pendulum clock
Walt Disney's "The Sword in the Stone" is released
Rod Serling, American screenwriter and TV host (The Twilight Zone; Night Gallery), born in Syracuse, New York (d. 1975)
Robert Ripley, collector of odd facts (Believe it or Not), born in Santa Rosa, California (d. 1949)
AstronomerAnders Celsiusintroduces Centigrade temperature scale
NASA Apollo 8 crew broadcast while orbiting the moon amd read passages from the Bible to celebrate Christmas
Return of Halley's comet 1st sighted by Johann Georg Palitzsch
"To Kill a Mockingbird", a film adaptation of the novel byHarper Lee, directed by Robert Mulligan and starringGregory Peck, is released (Gregory Peck - Best Actor Academy Awards 1963)
Tommy Chambers (Scotland) finishes 51 yr cycle tour (799,405 miles)
Baltimore RavensLamar Jacksonbreaks NFL career rushing record for quarterbacks in 31-2 win over the Texans in Houston; he runs for 87 yards, pushing his career rushing total to 6,110
New Orleans running back Alvin Kamara equals Ernie Nevers 1929 NFL record of 6 touchdowns in a match in the Saints' 52-33 win over the Minnesota Vikings at the Mercedes-Benz Superdome
Longest game in NFL history as Miami Dolphins beat KC Chiefs, 27-24 in 2OT in AFL playoff game; duration: 82 minutes and 40 seconds

Detroit's future Hockey Hall of Fame right wingGordie Howepicks up a Xmas hat-trick & 3 assists in Red Wings' 8-1 win over NY Rangers; most points he scores in a single game in entire 26-year NHL career
17-year old Liverpudlian Richard Starkey (later known asRingo Starr) receives his first drum set as a Christmas gift from his stepfather
Patriotic march "Stars & Stripes Forever" is written byJohn Philip Sousa
Coronation Stone, taken from Scone in Scotland by Edward I in 1296, stolen from Westminster Abbey & smuggled back to Scotland
Avalanche of lava kills 150 from Ruapehu volcano in New Zealand
The ill-fated Beagle 2 probe which was released from the Mars Express Spacecraft on December 19, disappears shortly before its scheduled landing.
During KingGeorge V's Christmas dinner speech his chair collapses
Montgomery Ward introduces Rudolph the 9th red-nosed reindeer
First known Christmas carol, "Silent Night, Holy Night" (Stille Nacht, Heilige Nacht'), is sung in Austria
StFrancis of Assisiassembles 1st Nativity scene (Greccio, Italy)
Leonard Bernsteinconducts Beethoven's Symphony No. 9 in East Berlin's Schauspielhaus to celebrate fall of the Berlin Wall. Broadcast worldwide to audience of 100 million.
Ncuti Gatwa makes his debut as the fifteenth incarnation of the Doctor in "Doctor Who", with Millie Gibson as his companion
Mt, Van Hoevenberg bobsled run at Lake Placid, NY opens; first American bobsled run open to public
William & Ellen Craft escape from slavery in Georgia, arriving in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; later move to Boston where they become abolitionists
Joseph Bologne, French violinist, conductor, composer, and champion fencer, born in Baillif, Guadeloupe, French West Indies (d. 1799)
Zora Arkus-Duntov, Belgian-American automotive engineer, known as "the Father of the Corevette", born in Brussels, Belgium (d. 1996)
Knicks basketball forward Bernard King scores 60 points, but New York loses, 120-114 to the New Jersey Nets on Xmas Day at Madison Square Garden
West Indies wrap up 6 wicket defeat of Australia on first Christmas Day of Test cricket, the 3rd day of 3rd Test at the Adelaide Oval
English earls declare Danish king Sweyn Forkbeard the first Viking king of England, disposing Ethelred the Unready
William the Conqueroris crowned King of England at Westminster Abbey, completing the Norman conquest of England
Pope Leo III crowns Charles the Great (Charlemagne), Roman Emperor
St Paul's Cathedral, designed by SirChristopher Wren, is officially declared complete after 35 years construction, by the British parliament

Queen Elizabeth IImakes her first Christmas Day broadcast, thanking her subjects for their "loyalty and affection" and promising to carry on the tradition, which she does every year of her reign except 1969
George CukorannouncesVivien Leighwill play Scarlett O'Hara in "Gone With The Wind"
1st live telecast of Christmas Parade at the EPCOT Centre, Disney World Florida
Taiwan passes Human Rights laws (Day of Earth Law)
Bishop Karol Wojtyla (future PopeJohn Paul II) celebrates outdoor Mass at midnight of Christmas morning to demonstrate the need for a Catholic church in the community of Nowa Huta
Jupiter Hammon, an African American slave, composes poetry broadside "An Evening Thought" (1st poetry published by an African American 1761)
Battle of Tucapel: Mapuche rebels under Lautaro defeats the Spanish conquistadors and executes the governor of Chile Pedro de Valdivia
First television adaptation ofCharles Dickens' "A Christmas Carol" debuts in American syndication; low budget 30-minute production stars Taylor Holmes and is narrated by Vincent Price
New York's Metropolitan Opera broadcasts an entire live opera performance for the first time - Englebert Humperdinck's "Hänsel und Gretel" over NBC radio
Handel's "Messiah", premieres in the US in Boston, performed by The Handel & Haydn Society
English KingEdward IVholds a lavish Christmas feast for 2,000 people at Eltham Palace
Sony brings transistor TV 8-301 to the market
First indoor baseball game played at fairgrounds in Philadelphia; 2,000 watch the Uptowners beat the Downtowners, 6-1
40,000 watch Union army men play baseball at Hilton Head, South Carolina
Louisiana & Arkansas are first states to observe Christmas as holiday
